title: How to invite to a board giving View-only access
excerpt: Hello - is there a way to invite internal users to a board, giving them view-only access, where they are still able to “open events” to view notes, item cards, files, etc. I want them to be able to see everything, just n…

posts.author: nbratton (Nicole Bratton)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello - is there a way to invite internal users to a board, giving them view-only access, where they are still able to “open events” to view notes, item cards, files, etc. I want them to be able to see everything, just not allow them to edit or change anything on the board. Right now the only thing I’ve seen is to give them the “sharable link,” which does allow them to view the board, however, they are not able to expand events (the comment icon area) where they can view notes made and the item card

posts.author: WaxConsultingGroup (Amanda)
posts.cookedHtml:
Do you have an INVITE button in the upper right corner of your board? First go into your board and set permissions to view only for everyone but the owners of the board, and then click the INVITE button and add internal users to the board.

Let us know if that helps!

posts.author: nbratton (Nicole Bratton)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks so much for this! I think I’ve got it figured out and can close this one. It seems I can be the owner with all access, then can limit others, but those others must have the same permissions (or also be designated as an owner). I was hoping I could have varying degrees of permissions for the people I invited; I stay as owner, then others could have some editing capabilities without changing structure, or edit only what you’re assigned to, then others as view only. For now, I am the only owner, then everyone else invited can “write updates on events only.”

posts.author: ellen05898 (Ellen Hogan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Nicole Bratton:

Hello - is there a way to invite internal users to a board, giving them view-only access, where they are still able to “open events” to view notes, item cards, files, etc. I want them to be able to see everything, just not allow them to edit or change anything on the board. Right now the only thing I’ve seen is to give them the “sharable link,” which does allow them to view the board, however, they are not able to expand events (the comment icon area) where they can view notes made and the item card

Hello,
To give internal users view-only access while allowing them to open events and view notes, item cards, and files, you need to:

  1. Invite Users with View-Only Permissions: Ensure they are added with view-only permissions.
  2. Adjust Board Settings: Check the board settings to enable viewing of detailed elements like notes and item cards for view-only users.

This setup should allow them to see everything without editing capabilities.

Best Regards,
Ellen Hogan

posts.author: WaxConsultingGroup (Amanda)
posts.cookedHtml: Nicole, you can also set column view/edit permissions as another layer! You can set it up where users can only edit the things assigned to them. Your upper level permissions might overrule that though, I’m not sure.
